description_long: This object is a vintage-style record player with a rectangular, brown case and a hinged lid that opens upward. The main body features a large, black circular turntable platter and a straight, beige tonearm extending over it. A prominent, faceted golden horn sits beside the main unit, reminiscent of early gramophones, and appears detachable. The case has a smooth, matte finish, and the horn is polygonal with a wide opening. No visible control knobs or buttons are present on the exterior, and the stylus detail is minimal or absent. The overall design is geometric and stylized, evoking a classic, retro aesthetic suitable for display or decorative use in urban environments.
description: A stylized, vintage record player with a brown rectangular case, black platter, beige tonearm, and a detachable golden horn.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open lid, black platter, beige tonearm, and the golden horn placed beside the main unit.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the polygonal horn attached to the side and the open lid, with the platter and tonearm visible.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the closed brown lid and the horn extending from the side, with minimal detail of the platter or tonearm.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the horn attached, the open lid, and a clear view of the platter and tonearm arrangement.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and black, occupying a significant portion of the main body. It has a smooth, flat surface with a small central spindle, and no visible grooves or patterns.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, straight, and beige, extending from the side of the case over the platter. It is of moderate length, with a simple, blocky design and no visible joints or mechanisms. The tonearm appears to be resting over the platter.
attribute_dust_cover: A hinged, solid brown lid acts as a dust cover, attached to the back of the case. It is opaque, non-transparent, and currently open, revealing the platter and tonearm. No handles or hinges are visibly detailed.
